frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: Combining symbolic features into novel composite representations

frameworks.name: linear combination of element codes
frameworks.one_line: Composite neural patterns are predicted as sums of element patterns

frameworks.name: high- vs low-dimensional neural geometry
frameworks.one_line: Contrasts parallel high-D codes against low-D spatially anchored codes

core_question: Which regional neural codes support compositional generalization from symbolic features?
key_assumptions: Composite stimuli should be predictable as linear combinations of element representations
Symbolic features (shape, color) can be cleanly individuated
BOLD pattern geometry reflects underlying representational format
manipulations: Combine two discrete symbolic features (shape and color)
Compare familiar elements vs novel composite stimuli
Region-by-region pattern analysis across hippocampus, vmPFC, PPC, V1
key_findings: Hippocampus encoded elementary visual attributes in a high-dimensional parallel format
vmPFC, PPC, and V1 patterns for composites were predictable as linear combinations of element signals
vmPFC composition occurred in a high-dimensional format
PPC and V1 composition used a low-dimensional spatial response-consistent reference frame
interpretations: Hippocampus supports flexible individuation of elementary features
Compositional generalization arises through region-specific linear composition formats
